homepage.heroHeadline: Solar Power Built for San Diego
homepage.heroSubline: Custom solar solutions designed for Southern California homes. Harness our abundant sunshine while protecting against coastal corrosion and wildfire risks.
homepage.aboutTitle: Horizon Solar: San Diego's Solar Specialists
aboutText: Founded in 2012, Horizon Solar began with a mission to bring reliable renewable energy to San Diego neighborhoods. Our team of local installers understands the unique challenges of coastal installations and inland heat.

We combine premium equipment with precision engineering to maximize energy production in San Diego's microclimates. Every system is tailored to your roof type, whether it's a La Jolla cottage or Scripps Ranch estate.
As active members of the San Diego Green Building Council, we're committed to sustainable growth that preserves our region's natural beauty while reducing energy costs for homeowners.

valueProps.title: Climate-Adapted Designs
valueProps.description: Systems engineered for salt air corrosion resistance and wildfire-prone areas with specialized mounting solutions.

valueProps.title: Local Expertise
valueProps.description: 12 years serving San Diego County means we know every permit process and utility requirement.

valueProps.title: Premium Warranties
valueProps.description: 25-year performance guarantees backed by manufacturers we've vetted for coastal durability.

valueProps.title: Neighborhood Knowledge
valueProps.description: We've installed on every roof type from North Park bungalows to Del Mar contemporary homes.

homepage.ctaSubline: Get a free assessment of your home's energy savings with our San Diego-specific calculator.

services.name: Residential Solar Installation
services.shortDescription: Complete home solar systems designed for San Diego's architecture and climate.
services.description:
From Spanish-tile roofs in Coronado to flat-top midcentury homes in Clairemont, we design solar arrays that complement your architecture while maximizing energy production. Our coastal installations use marine-grade hardware to prevent salt corrosion, while inland systems incorporate cooling ventilation for peak summer performance.

We handle everything from SDGE interconnection paperwork to historic district approvals in neighborhoods like Bankers Hill. Each installation includes bird-proofing measures tailored to local wildlife and custom monitoring that tracks your system's performance against San Diego weather patterns.
process: Step 1: Site evaluation assessing roof condition, sun exposure, and local shading factors
Step 2: Custom system design with 3D modeling showing panel placement
Step 3: Permitting through San Diego County and your local jurisdiction
Step 4: Precision installation with minimal disruption to your household
benefits: Increased home value in San Diego's competitive market
Protection against SDGE rate increases
Reduced carbon footprint aligned with California's clean energy goals
Eligibility for local rebates and federal tax credits

faq.question: How do you handle HOA approvals in communities like La Jolla?
faq.answer: We've successfully navigated approvals with all major San Diego HOAs, providing aesthetic renderings and technical specifications that meet community standards while maximizing efficiency.

faq.question: What's different about solar installations near the coast?
faq.answer: We use stainless steel hardware and anti-corrosion coatings on all coastal installations, plus additional wind-load calculations for beachfront properties.

services.name: Solar Battery Storage
services.shortDescription: Energy resilience solutions for San Diego's power outages and time-of-use rates.
services.description:
With increasing wildfire risks in East County and planned outages in North County, battery storage provides essential backup power for San Diego homes. We install Tesla Powerwalls and other premium systems sized to your household's needs, whether you're in a downtown condo or Rancho Santa Fe estate.

Our storage solutions automatically switch during outages and optimize for SDGE's time-of-use rates. We configure systems to power critical circuits like medical equipment or pool pumps, with remote monitoring through our San Diego-based support team. Coastal installations include humidity-controlled enclosures, while inland systems feature enhanced cooling for desert-edge communities.
process: Step 1: Energy audit analyzing your usage patterns and outage risks
Step 2: Battery sizing calculation based on SDGE rate plans
Step 3: Custom electrical integration with your existing panel
Step 4: Smart programming for automatic outage response
benefits: Continuous power during PSPS outages in fire-risk areas
Maximized savings under SDGE's latest rate structures
Whole-home or selective circuit backup options
Increased independence from the grid

faq.question: How long will batteries last during a blackout?
faq.answer: Most San Diego homes see 1-3 days of essential power, depending on usage. We design systems to prioritize your needs, whether it's keeping the fridge cold or AC running.

faq.question: Do batteries help with coastal marine layer mornings?
faq.answer: Absolutely. Storage captures excess afternoon sun to power your home through June gloom mornings when panels produce less.

services.name: Solar Maintenance
services.shortDescription: Specialized care for San Diego solar systems facing unique environmental challenges.
services.description:
San Diego's combination of intense UV exposure, occasional Santa Ana winds, and coastal salt spray demands specialized solar maintenance. Our bi-annual service checks go beyond basic cleaning to include corrosion inspections on coastal systems, torque checks on mountings in windy areas like Torrey Pines, and thermal imaging to detect hot spots in East County's extreme heat.

We offer tailored maintenance plans based on your location - beach communities receive extra attention to salt buildup, while inland homes get focused cooling system checks before summer. Our technicians are certified for all major brands and keep current on San Diego-specific issues through ongoing training at Mesa College's renewable energy program.
process: Step 1: Comprehensive system health check with performance analysis
Step 2: Environment-specific cleaning (salt removal, dust clearing, etc.)
Step 3: Electrical safety inspection and component testing
Step 4: Detailed report with efficiency benchmarks for San Diego conditions
benefits: Prevents costly repairs from undetected environmental damage
Maintains peak efficiency through seasonal changes
Extends system lifespan beyond warranty periods
Peace of mind with local same-day emergency service

faq.question: How often should coastal systems be serviced?
faq.answer: We recommend quarterly inspections for homes within 2 miles of the ocean due to accelerated corrosion risks from salt air.

faq.question: Do you handle SDGE meter maintenance too?
faq.answer: Yes, we coordinate with SDGE for any meter-related issues and ensure your net metering system communicates properly with their grid.

services.name: Solar Panel Repair
services.shortDescription: Rapid response repairs for San Diego solar systems damaged by local conditions.
services.description:
When marine layer condensation causes electrical issues or Santa Ana winds loosen racking, San Diego solar owners need fast, knowledgeable repairs. Our team specializes in diagnosing and fixing problems unique to our region, from salt-corroded connectors in Ocean Beach to heat-warped components in El Cajon.

We maintain an extensive inventory of compatible parts at our Kearny Mesa warehouse for quick repairs. For historic homes in areas like Mission Hills, we source period-appropriate replacement materials that maintain architectural integrity. Emergency service calls prioritize households with medical equipment or elderly residents during extended outages.
process: Step 1: Remote diagnostics via your system's monitoring data
Step 2: On-site troubleshooting with specialized testing equipment
Step 3: Transparent repair options with upfront pricing
Step 4: Post-repair performance verification
benefits: Same-day service for most San Diego County locations
Repairs that address root causes, not just symptoms
Technicians familiar with all local roof types
Direct communication with manufacturers for warranty claims

faq.question: What's the most common repair in coastal areas?
faq.answer: Junction box resealing accounts for 40% of our coastal repairs - salt air penetrates compromised seals faster than manufacturers anticipate.

faq.question: How do you repair tile roofs without causing damage?
faq.answer: Our Spanish tile specialists use historic preservation techniques to remove and replace tiles without breakage, a skill honed on Coronado's historic district homes.

services.name: Solar Water Heating
services.shortDescription: Efficient hot water solutions leveraging San Diego's abundant sunshine.
services.description:
While most think of photovoltaics, solar water heating remains incredibly effective in San Diego's climate. We install high-efficiency evacuated tube systems that provide 80% of a typical household's hot water needs, ideal for large families in Scripps Ranch or vacation rentals in Pacific Beach.

Our designs account for local water quality - we install scale prevention systems for hard water areas and corrosion-resistant materials near the coast. Tankless hybrid systems combine with existing gas heaters for endless hot water during June gloom periods. For historic homes where visible roof equipment is restricted, we offer discrete ground-mounted options that still capture San Diego's reliable sunshine.
process: Step 1: Hot water usage analysis and solar exposure assessment
Step 2: System design matching your household size and architecture
Step 3: Installation with minimal plumbing disruption
Step 4: Performance tuning to your water usage patterns
benefits: Immediate reduction in gas bills
Eligible for additional California rebates beyond solar PV
Longer lifespan than conventional water heaters
Reduces strain on your main electrical system

faq.question: How does this work with San Diego's mild winters?
faq.answer: Our systems maintain efficiency even during cooler months - the evacuated tube technology captures diffuse sunlight effectively during marine layer periods.

faq.question: Can it handle a vacation home's variable usage?
faq.answer: Yes, we install vacation-mode systems that prevent stagnation during unoccupied periods while providing instant hot water when you return.

reviews.name: Mikael R.
reviews.text: Horizon designed a perfect system for our 1940s Craftsman in North Park - they custom-fabricated low-profile mounts that preserved our roof's historic lines while maximizing production. Even during June gloom, we're producing more than projected.

reviews.name: Sophia K.
reviews.text: When the power went out during last October's Santa Anas, our Powerwall kept the lights on for 3 days straight. Horizon's team configured it to prioritize our home office and refrigerator - exactly what we needed during the outage.

reviews.name: Carlos T.
reviews.text: Living in Ocean Beach means constant salt exposure. Horizon's quarterly maintenance caught corroding connectors before they failed. Their technician even noticed seagull nesting attempts under our panels and installed humane deterrents.

reviews.name: Danielle P.
reviews.text: After a windstorm loosened panels on our Del Mar home, Horizon had a crew out same-day. They discovered the original installers used inland-grade hardware - replaced everything with marine-certified parts at no extra charge.

reviews.name: Rajesh V.
reviews.text: Our Scripps Ranch home went from $200/month gas bills to $35 after Horizon's solar water heater install. The system seamlessly integrates with our existing tankless heater for those rare cloudy stretches.

reviews.name: Lisa M.
reviews.text: Horizon navigated La Jolla's strict HOA requirements beautifully - our panels are invisible from the street but produce 120% of our needs. Their knowledge of coastal microclimates resulted in better production than three competing proposals.

service-areas.title: San Diego Solar Service Areas | Horizon Solar
service-areas.description: We provide premium solar installations throughout San Diego County, including coastal communities and inland neighborhoods.

serviceAreas.content: Horizon Solar proudly serves Chula Vista, a vibrant city with a population of approximately 275,000 residents. Known for its suburban homes built primarily in the 1970s and 1980s, Chula Vista offers ample rooftop space for solar panel installations. The mild year-round climate makes it an ideal location for harnessing solar energy, ensuring maximum efficiency and savings for homeowners. Our team specializes in tailoring solar solutions to fit the unique architectural styles and energy needs of Chula Vista residents. With rising energy costs, now is the perfect time to switch to solar and take advantage of local incentives. Horizon Solar is committed to helping Chula Vista homeowners reduce their carbon footprint while enjoying significant savings on their electricity bills.
serviceAreas.localContext: Chula Vista features 1970s-80s suburban homes and enjoys a mild year-round climate, making it a prime location for solar energy.

serviceAreas.content: Horizon Solar is proud to serve the beautiful beach community of Carlsbad, home to around 115,000 residents. Known for its charming cottages and coastal vibe, Carlsbad is an excellent place for solar energy adoption. The mild year-round climate ensures consistent solar energy production, allowing homeowners to maximize their savings. Our team understands the unique needs of Carlsbad residents, from preserving the aesthetic appeal of beach cottages to navigating local permitting processes. Solar panels not only reduce energy costs but also contribute to Carlsbad's commitment to sustainability. Let Horizon Solar help you make the switch to clean, renewable energy and enjoy the benefits of solar power in this picturesque coastal city.
serviceAreas.localContext: Carlsbad is a beach community with cottages and a mild year-round climate, ideal for solar energy solutions.

serviceAreas.content: Horizon Solar is excited to serve Escondido, a city of approximately 152,000 residents known for its 1960s-70s ranch-style homes.
